evals.prompt: Set up conventional commits and semantic versioning in this repo. I want automated version bumps based on my commit messages.
evals.expected_output: Installs semantic-release and plugins, creates .releaserc.json with correct plugin order, adds release script to package.json. Asks about commitlint/husky and CI workflow.
expectations: semantic-release is installed as a devDependency
.releaserc.json is created with @semantic-release/commit-analyzer using conventionalcommits preset
Plugin order in .releaserc.json is: commit-analyzer, release-notes-generator, changelog, npm, github, git
A 'release' script is added to package.json
The user is asked whether they want commitlint + husky enforcement
The user is asked whether they want a GitHub Actions workflow

evals.prompt: I want to add semantic versioning to this project. I think we already have standard-version set up from a while back, not sure though. Can you check and set up whatever we need?
evals.expected_output: Detects existing standard-version setup, reports findings to user, asks for confirmation before removal, then installs semantic-release.
expectations: The existing versioning tool is detected and reported to the user
The user is asked for confirmation before any removal happens
After removal (if confirmed), semantic-release is installed

evals.prompt: What commit message should I use if I'm making a breaking change to our API? I renamed several public endpoints.
evals.expected_output: Explains the BREAKING CHANGE format, shows examples with both ! syntax and footer syntax, explains this triggers a MAJOR version bump.
expectations: The response explains that breaking changes trigger a MAJOR version bump
At least one example commit message is provided
Both the ! prefix syntax and BREAKING CHANGE footer syntax are mentioned
